evals.prompt: I need to add a real-time streaming chat endpoint to my FastAPI app. It should accept a POST request with a prompt, then stream back tokens one by one as server-sent events. The client will be a browser using EventSource. Show me how to implement this properly.
evals.expected_output: An SSE endpoint using FastAPI's native EventSourceResponse from fastapi.sse (not sse-starlette), with an async generator function, yielding ServerSentEvent objects with event types for tokens and a done signal. Should use POST method and note that browser EventSource doesn't support POST (so fetch is needed client-side).
expectations: Uses `from fastapi.sse import EventSourceResponse, ServerSentEvent` (not sse-starlette or custom implementation)
Endpoint is defined with `async def` (not regular def)
Uses POST method for the chat endpoint
Mentions or handles the [DONE] sentinel or end-of-stream signal

evals.prompt: How should I run my FastAPI app? I want to run it in development with auto-reload and also for production. What server should I use and what commands do I run?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend Granian (not uvicorn) with `uv add granian` and `uv run granian --interface asgi main:app` commands, showing both dev (--reload) and production (--workers N) variants. Should NOT recommend uvicorn or fastapi dev.
expectations: Recommends Granian as the server (not uvicorn, not `fastapi dev`)
Shows `--interface asgi` flag in the command
Does not recommend uvicorn as an alternative

evals.prompt: Create a complete async CRUD API for a `Product` resource using FastAPI. It should have endpoints to list, get, create, and delete products. Show me the database setup, the model, schemas, repository, and routes.
evals.expected_output: Full async implementation using psycopg (no ORM): AsyncConnectionPool with get_pg_connection(), PostgresTableModel for the model, pg_* helpers for CRUD, repository methods all async, Pydantic schemas for request/response, route handlers all async def WITHOUT Depends(get_db) since repos manage their own connections.
expectations: All route handlers use `async def` (no sync def routes)
Uses psycopg (AsyncConnectionPool or get_pg_connection) — NOT SQLAlchemy
Repository methods are all `async def` and call `get_pg_connection()` directly
Route handlers do NOT take a `db` session as a Depends parameter
Pydantic schemas or models defined for request/response
Uses pg_* helpers (pg_insert, pg_retrieve, pg_delete) or psycopg cursor for queries
